title: Editing the Command Line
subtitle: {key:Ctrl-B}, {key:Ctrl-E}, {key:Ctrl-W}, {key:Ctrl-U} — readline-flavored.
summary: Inside the : prompt you have a small editing keyset: Ctrl-B/Ctrl-E for line ends, Ctrl-W to delete word, Ctrl-U to clear, arrow keys, and Ctrl-Left/Ctrl-Right for word jumps.

blocks.text: The {key::} prompt is its own mini-editor. The keys are familiar from Insert mode and from readline.

rows: Insert register contents (as in Insert mode)

blocks.title: The Word Under Cursor — Ctrl-R Ctrl-W
blocks.text:
{key:Ctrl-R}{key:Ctrl-W} from the command line inserts the **word under the cursor in the buffer**. Combine with substitute: position cursor on a name, then `:%s/<Ctrl-R><Ctrl-W>/newname/g` — type once, get the word inserted automatically.

Variants:

  • {key:Ctrl-R}{key:Ctrl-A} — WORD under cursor.
  • {key:Ctrl-R}{key:Ctrl-F} — filename under cursor.
  • {key:Ctrl-R}{key:Ctrl-L} — entire current line.

All save typing when the thing you want to type is already in the buffer.
